lessons: When a high-conviction position drops 6-10% in a broad sector selloff with no company-specific fundamental catalyst (no earnings miss, no contract loss, no regulatory threat, no management change), treat it as market noise and hold. Trigger: >6% single-day decline + no fundamental catalyst identified + RSI post-drop 40-50 = hold, do not sell.

lessons: Lesson 13: When a position exceeds its stated price target by more than 10% but RSI remains below 70 (not yet technically overbought), do not trim solely on the basis of the price target breach. Price targets are working estimates of fair value — not hard automatic exits. When only one of the two required signals fires (price exceeded target) without the second (RSI overbought confirmation), the correct action is to update the target upward to reflect market-confirmed and thesis-confirmed fair value, then hold.
